_comment: Schema for comms/claude_strategy_scores.jsonl — the durable, repo-tracked log of Claude's per-DECISION (per-order-package) scores. APPEND-ONLY JSONL: one JSON object per graded order package, keyed by `order_package_id` so it cross-references trade_journal.db::order_packages (and, when the package filled, `linked_trade_id` -> trades.id). Supersedes the trade-keyed comms/claude_trade_scores.jsonl (operator decision 2026-05-25: the score belongs to the STRATEGY DECISION — the order package is what a strategy emits when it decides to act — independent of whether/how it filled). The file may carry a single leading {"_meta": ...} line (skip rows without `order_package_id`). Written by .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md and the retroactive backfiller scripts/ops/score_order_packages.py (which holds the canonical rubric in _grade_package). `decision_grade_score` mirrors the review_journal family mapping (A/B/C/D/F -> 4/3/2/1/0). This file is documentation only — not validated as a JSON Schema.

record.linked_trade_id: int or null — trades.id when the package filled; null for shadow / never-filled packages
record.reviewed_at: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp — when Claude graded this decision
record.strategy_name: vwap | ict_scalp_5m | trend_donchian | fade_breakout_4h | squeeze_breakout_4h | turtle_soup | ...
record.executed: bool — true when linked_trade_id is set (the decision actually filled)
record.created_at: ISO-8601 — when the order package was created (the decision timestamp)
record.pnl: float or null — realised PnL of the linked trade when executed; null for shadow/unfilled
record.exit_reason: tp_cross | sl_hit | trail | reconciler_filled | manual | null (close_reason/exit_reason)
record.entry_quality: optimal | acceptable | late | early | should_skip | unknown
record.exit_quality: optimal | tp_appropriate | sl_appropriate | premature_exit | held_too_long | unknown (unknown for shadow/unfilled)
record.risk_management: correct | oversize | undersize | sl_too_tight | sl_too_wide | unknown
record.rationale: <= 240 chars — why this grade given the package's signal_logic + (when filled) outcome
record.alternative_action: <= 160 chars — what we'd do differently next time, or 'none'
